feed.title: Polly's Mind Palace
feed.subtitle: A minimal, responsive, and powerful Jekyll theme for presenting professional writing.

entry.title: Hash (Python)
entry.summary: Original Problem Create a dataset. Achieve three functions: Insert: put one element into the dataset. Delete: delete all the corresponding elements from the dataset. Search: check the dataset to search for certain element. If it exists, return 1; if not, return 0. The real purpose of this problem is asking you to create a hash table. If not, the time complexibility will become $O(...

entry.title: Draw A UML Class Diagram
entry.summary: Original Problem: The Shenzhou VI spacecraft is one of the Shenzhou spacecraft series, consisting of orbital module, re-entry capsule and escape tower. The re-entry capsule is where astronauts can drive the spacecraft, while the orbital module is where they work and have a rest. In case of emergency, astronauts can go to the escape tower for safety. The spacecraft is flanked by wings of sol...

entry.title: Full Permutation with Recursion
entry.summary: Origianl Problem Give you a number $n$, print the full permutation of the number list $(1\to n)$. My Solution Here we use recursion. My source code is listed below: #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ Permutation with recursion Written by Polly Zhou, Tsinghua University E-mail: pollyjoe2003@gmail.com Solution: Fix one element, and permute the remaining elements. Acknowledgement: C...

entry.title: Bugs I Encountered (Week 3)
entry.summary: Problom 1 Wrong number Original Problem: Give you a string, extract all the negative numbers from the string. e.g. (Source code: THU OJ) Input: This line contains 1 integer "one". This line contains no integer. 233, next line is empty. If you got -123.456, you should output 123 and 456. Any digit string are considered integers, like 0123456 and 007. You may also need to consider cases lik...

entry.title: Binary Search Tree (Python)
entry.summary: Original Problem Given a number list, you can confirm a binary search tree. Now given two specified elements, you have to find the lowest common ancestor. My Solution Source code is listed below: #!/usr/bin/env python3 # Minimum common ancestors of binary seaarch tree # Special acknowledgement for Bingchuan Wei. # After discussed this practice with him, I learned basic binary search tree ...
